summary: Elderly patients with oropharyngeal cancer are at higher risk of undertreatment because their diminished physical resilience and greater comorbidities reduce tolerability of intensive therapies such as chemoradiotherapy. This leads to underutilized standard-of-care approaches despite potential benefit from them.
